results.title: Your Money (Captioned)
results.brief_description: We all need money, we all use money. In the ever-changing financial climate, it’s becoming more and more important to have a sound understanding of money management. The difference between those of us who achieve their financial goals and those of us who don’t, can be measured in a few simple rules. “Your money” is an easy to understand step by step guide to making better financial decisions.

results.title: Your heritage
results.creator_credit: Cinemakers New York and National Trust for Historic Preservation
results.brief_description: A plea for preservation of historically significant American architecture, this film won the awards for the best TV commercial at the 7th Chicago International Film Festival and the TV Commercials Festival, 1971.

results.title: Your hair and scalp
results.creator_credit: Ken Woodward, Hindle Marriott Edgar and World Wide
results.brief_description: Animated diagrams showing the structure of hair, the process of growth and how to care for one’s hair. Natural variations of hair are shown and artificial treatments. The importance of cleanliness and good grooming in maintaining both health and appearance is emphasised.

results.title: Your friend the soil: keep it - or lose it
results.brief_description: Partly animated and partly live action, this film shows how the nation’s food production depends on good farming practices such as contour ploughing and strip cropping, resulting in abundant crops, healthy farm animals and therefore, well-stocked shops. Discusses soil erosion and bad farming techniques. Suitable for primary level.

results.title: Your credit is good
results.brief_description: The film opens with a sequence of artwork describing how instalment buying works. Then the message is driven home that no matter what you think, you’ve been promised verbally, no matter how good or easy it sounds - you pay what it says on the paper.

results.title: Your clothing can burn
results.creator_credit: Alfred Higgins and Alfred Higgins Productions
results.brief_description: Demonstrates the correct as well as incorrect procedures for dealing with clothing whenit is ignited. Uses laboratory experiments to show the importance of selecting clothing and fabrics that resist fire.

results.title: Your children walking
results.creator_credit: Alex Strasser, Brian Smith and Realist Films
results.brief_description: Stresses the importance of adopting good foot habits and wearing suitable shoes and socks in childhood to develop strong, healthy feet. Studies the physiology of the foot.
